title: Why Quantum Computing Still Isn’t Close To Its ChatGPT Moment
why:
- Despite recent hype and the debut of consumer-facing quantum chatbots, quantum computing is not expected to deliver significant enterprise value until the end of the decade even by the most optimistic estimates.
- Quantum computing is fundamentally different from AI in that it is specialized for only a very small subset of mathematically hard problems, rather than being a versatile, general-purpose technology.
- Many of the key solutions quantum computing promises have not yet been realized or are still far in the future, indicating the field remains in a nascent stage.
